narrative:
Diney is a pivotal Centre-Back for Portugal, boasting an overall rating of 7.35/10. This places him among the top 1% of centre-backs at this tournament. His rating range is 6.98–7.73, reflecting the current match sample size.

At this World Cup, Diney demonstrates strong defensive prowess, averaging 9.0 clearances per 90 minutes. He also contributes 2.538 tackles and 0.923 interceptions per 90 minutes, highlighting his defensive work rate. His offensive contribution is minimal, with expected goals per 90 minutes at just 0.042 and expected assists at 0.004. This shows a clear focus on defensive duties rather than attacking phases.

Diney serves as a primary defensive anchor for Portugal, having played 390 minutes and anchoring the backline. His role is clearly to nullify opposition attacks, not to initiate them. Scouts and fans should continue to watch for his consistent defensive output and leadership in the remaining matches.
